title: EmbodiedMusic
description: Keep in Survey: robot music is niche but still testable if scoped as action/contact-to-MIDI before full audio generation.
summary: Survey-stage project from Notion EmbodiedMusic and robot rhythm ideas: connect physical action, tactile/contact timing, motion capture, symbolic music, and audio generation so embodied agents can perform or generate music grounded in real instrument interactions.

details.text: Core idea: physical action prompt / PAP for action-conditioned music generation over 3D instrument assets. The minimum contribution should map action/contact trajectories to MIDI or audio events before attempting full music generation.

Data route from Notion: tactile glove, camera, microphone, AMT/MIDI, rule mapping, and DDSP/diffusion audio generation. Existing music datasets and differentiable audio tools should be treated as output/evaluation support, while the embodied novelty is action/contact-to-sound grounding.

details: Open Notion items folded here: robot musical rhythm from rapper/body-motion data, embodied instrument assets, action-conditioned generation, evaluation by timing/pitch/F1/audio quality, and Week-1 task planning already written inside Notion.

details.text: Notion migration addendum 2026-05-31: the detailed Notion plan already defines PAP = timestamp, instrument part, contact location, velocity, force, plus AMT/MIDI bootstrapping, MAESTRO, tactile glove/camera/mic sync, DDSP/diffusion backends, and pitch/timing/loudness/FAD-style evaluation. Keep as Survey until one instrument and one metric set are chosen.

risks_decisions: This is interesting but off the main robotics manipulation pipeline; keep it survey unless there is a concrete dataset or demo owner.
Need separate symbolic and audio objectives. MIDI/event correctness is easier to verify than generated audio quality.
Robot rhythm from human body motion may be a different paper from contact-conditioned instrument performance; do not merge them prematurely.
First deliverable should be a problem formulation and related-work map, not a model build.

Survey disposition 2026-05-31: keep, because RoboPianist/FürElise establish important baselines but do not close the general action-contact-to-symbolic-music problem for our glove/tactile/camera stack.

Next executable analysis: choose one minimal instrument/task, define timing and pitch/event metrics, and decide whether the dataset starts from MIDI supervision, microphone audio, or tactile/contact traces.
Archive if there is no measurable action-to-event objective and the idea drifts into generic music generation.

The rapper/body-motion rhythm idea is folded here as a possible rhythm-conditioning dataset, but should stay separate from contact-to-instrument-event grounding until the first problem formulation is written.
